problem.title: Restore IP Addresses
problem.description: A valid IP address consists of exactly four integers separated by single dots. Each integer is between 0 and 255 (inclusive) and cannot have leading zeros.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Backtracking
solutions.description: Use backtracking to place exactly three dots, trying segment lengths of 1–3 characters at each position. A segment is valid only if it has no leading zero (unless it is '0' itself) and its integer value is at most 255. When four segments have been placed and the entire string is consumed, a valid IP is recorded.
